summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 ships postinstall beacon scripts that exfiltrate host data over HTTP
details: Package executes `node run.js` as a postinstall lifecycle hook and ships beacon scripts (beacon31.js, beacon_linux.js) that combine child_process, fs, os, and http/https modules to gather host identifiers and environment variables and send them to remote endpoints. beacon31.js requires child_process/fs/https, reads files via fs.readFileSync, references process.env and process.platform, and issues https.request/GET calls. beacon_linux.js requires child_process/os/http, reads os.hostname() and os.platform(), and issues http.get / http.request POSTs. The postinstall hook causes this code to run automatically when a developer runs `npm install`, exposing installer environment variables, host metadata, and filesystem contents to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The package name (random-suffixed `metrics-pipeline-d8k2`) and file naming (`beacon*`) are consistent with a throwaway exfiltration package rather than a legitimate metrics library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
